items.question: Your team wants Claude to answer HR policy questions accurately. What pattern fits best?
options: Ask Claude from memory with no documents
Ground answers with uploaded policy text or retrieval, human review for edge cases
Use Claude only for image generation
items.answer: Ground answers with uploaded policy text or retrieval, human review for edge cases
items.explanation: Enterprise FAQ use cases need supplied context and human oversight.

items.question: Which is a poor primary Claude use case?
options: Drafting meeting summaries from pasted notes
Substituting a audited nightly SQL reconciliation pipeline with chat alone
items.answer: Substituting a audited nightly SQL reconciliation pipeline with chat alone
items.explanation: Bulk deterministic data processing belongs in code/SQL; Claude assists around the edges.

items.question: What does "human in the loop" mean for customer support drafts?
options: Agents review and edit suggested replies before customers see them
Customers prompt Claude directly without logging
items.answer: Agents review and edit suggested replies before customers see them
items.explanation: Support use cases augment agents; they do not remove accountability.

items.question: When is grounding (supplying source documents in the prompt) most important?
options: Answering questions about your company's internal policies or proprietary data
Choosing a font for a slide deck
items.answer: Answering questions about your company's internal policies or proprietary data
items.explanation: Policy and enterprise FAQ use cases need explicit source text—Claude cannot reliably know private facts from pretraining alone.
